text: Debugging is an essential skill for any programmer. A systematic approach helps you find and fix errors efficiently. First, try to identify the bug by reproducing the problem consistently. Next, use debugging tools like breakpoints to pause your code and inspect variables. Many developers also rely on logging—adding print statements or writing to a log file—to track the program’s flow and values. Instead of guessing, work step by step: check one possible cause at a time. This saves time and makes the process less frustrating. Once you find the issue, test your fix thoroughly. With practice, debugging becomes a logical puzzle rather than a headache.
